sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.2 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: A mixed identity\/ of a group$G$is, by definition, an identity of an algebraic system obtained from$G$by supplementing its signature by some set of 0-ary operations. One can develop a theory of mixed varieties of groups on the basis of this notion (see, for example, V.\,S.\,Anashin, Math. USSR Sbornik, 57 (1987), 171--182). Construct an example of a class of groups which is not a mixed variety, bu...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
